purpose: Determines whether the banking channel — the single most important severity discriminant — is active, loading, or dormant in real time
components: KRE (regional bank equities) sympathy with HY widening
Bank CDS spread levels and trend
CET1 ratio adequacy relative to loss exposure
CRE concentration at regional vs large banks (28-30% vs 6-8%)
CLO warehouse lines from regional banks as second-order channel
current_assessment: NOT ACTIVATED. KRE stable, bank CDS range-bound, CET1 13-14%. CRE concentration constitutes loading but not activation. Current capital buffers are a genuine structural break vs historical episodes (4-8% CET1).
limitations: Quantitative calibration of how much CET1 structural break reduces activation probability is unknown
CRE loss rates that would trigger activation at current capital levels not modeled
Regional bank heterogeneity (CRE concentration varies widely) not captured by aggregate CET1
Private credit risk transfer may have changed the banking channel's effective scope

purpose: Assesses whether the fiscal-to-profit channel is intact, sufficient, and sustainable for supporting credit-equity valuations
components: Kalecki-Lévy profit identity: Profits ≡ Investment + Government Deficit + Net Exports − Household Savings
Fiscal deficit level (current 6-7% GDP, ~$1.5-1.8T annually)
Quality differentiation: buffer sufficient for IG/upper HY, insufficient for CCC/leveraged loans
Godley sectoral balance constraint: both resolution paths lead toward instability
Deficit-term premium reflexive loop as endogenous destabilizer
current_assessment: Fully operational. S&P 500 EPS grew 8-10% annualized despite restrictive monetary policy. Configuration is genuinely unprecedented in credit-equity analogue set. Neither party has austerity mandate, DOGE impact is $100-200B/yr vs $2T+ deficit. Buffer is durable on 1-2 year horizon but structurally unsustainable per Godley.
limitations: No observable stopping rule to distinguish approaching instability from years of continued compression
Aggregate mechanism does not protect individual firm cash flows (Kalecki quality differentiation)
Japan parallel: fiscal support sustained economy but NOT asset prices — 60%+ equity decline despite deficits
Threshold of fiscal consolidation that cracks the floor not quantified
Political economy makes consolidation >2pp GDP unlikely but bond market reaction (à la UK gilt crisis) could force it

purpose: Estimates the probability and pathway of full credit-equity contagion chain completion from current structural loading
components: Banking channel activation status (primary severity discriminant)
Maturity wall arithmetic ($900B-$1.5T, 2025-2028)
CLO OC test CCC-bucket utilization (4-6.5% vs 7.5% threshold)
CLO reinvestment period expirations ($250-350B over 18 months)
Basis trade notional ($800B-$1T at 50-100x leverage)
Kalecki fiscal buffer status
Fed policy flexibility (inflation constraint is swing variable)
Episode analogue base rate (3/7 severe outcomes = ~35-40% unconditional)
current_assessment: Normal regime with elevated structural loading. ~35-40% unconditional completion probability (from analogue base rate), reducible to ~20-25% with operational Fed put or inflatable to ~40-50% with inflation-constrained Fed. P(inflation constrains Fed | credit stress emerges) is the most decision-relevant unknown. No current indicators suggest imminent transition.
limitations: Only 7 analogues — small sample for probability estimation
Structural changes (CET1 break, private credit, fiscal configuration) make historical base rates potentially unreliable
Conditional probabilities (joint probability of recession AND maturity wall AND constrained Fed) not rigorously estimated
0-for-4 base rate of maturity walls producing systemic stress in modern markets suggests maturity wall alone is insufficient

purpose: Identifies the current credit-equity correlation regime and provides early warning of transitions
components: Bimodal regime taxonomy: normal (0.3-0.4) vs crisis (0.7-0.9)
Early warning window: correlation rising from 0.3 to 0.5, lasting 2-5 months historically
Banking channel activation as primary transition trigger
Private credit opacity delaying measured correlation by 12-18 months
Structural bifurcation masking: equal-weight/cap-weight ratio as alternative diagnostic
Synthetic credit reverse causality contaminating high-frequency measurement
current_assessment: NORMAL REGIME. HY OAS at 350-420bp near cycle tights, VIX 13-18, KRE stable. generalist_01's claim of early stress decorrelation was refuted. Correct characterization: normal regime with elevated structural loading. Equal-weight/cap-weight divergence of ~15-20% suggests underlying stress not captured by aggregate indicators.
limitations: Early warning window may be compressing (2020 transition <1 month vs historical 2-5 months)
Private credit opacity means measured correlation may lag economic correlation by 12-18 months
Structural bifurcation between mega-cap and leveraged universe means aggregate measures systematically understate stress
Frequency-domain decomposition needed to separate genuine fundamental signal from synthetic credit contamination

purpose: Determines whether the maturity wall produces orderly refinancing or systemic stress, conditional on macro environment
components: Maturity arithmetic: $900B-$1.5T sub-IG, 2025-2028
Refinancing cost increase: 200-400bp for B-rated, representing 40-60% cash interest increase
True leverage exposure: 7.5-8.5x after EBITDA addback adjustment vs reported ~6x
Historical base rate: 2/2 walls coinciding with recession = systemic, 0/2 without recession = orderly
CLO demand withdrawal: reinvestment period expirations + potential OC test breach
ZIRP unavailability: prior defusing via amend-and-extend less feasible at current rates
current_assessment: Arithmetic is certain. Systemic outcome conditional on recession coincidence. Currently 0-for-4 in modern markets for producing systemic stress. Key conditional: if recession coincides with peak refinancing window AND CLO demand withdrawal AND constrained Fed, the wall becomes amplifier rather than orderly refinancing exercise.
limitations: 0-for-4 base rate suggests maturity walls are necessary but not sufficient for systemic stress
This time is different reasoning (higher rates) demands lower confidence
Share of borrowers above vs below Kalecki floor's effective altitude not quantified
CLO manager active trading partially softens the step-function framing of OC test breach

purpose: Demonstrates why both fiscal resolution paths (consolidation and continuation) lead toward credit-equity instability
components: Godley sectoral balance identity: Private + Government + Foreign ≡ 0
Path A (consolidation): reduces corporate profits via Kalecki identity, triggering credit cycle downshift within 4-6 quarters
Path B (continuation): generates $2T+ annual Treasury issuance, elevating term premium and loading basis trade channel
Deficit-term premium reflexive loop: deficit → issuance → term premium → higher interest → larger deficit
Non-linear deficit-term premium relationship: insensitive below 3-4% deficit/GDP, sensitive above
current_assessment: Current configuration is in the sensitive zone (6-7% deficit/GDP). Political economy makes Path A unlikely (neither party has austerity mandate). Path B is the default trajectory, with the reflexive loop as the endogenous destabilizer. The question is timing and trigger, not direction. The blended borrowing rate (~4.5%) vs nominal GDP growth (~5.5%) margin of ~100bp is historically thin.
limitations: US reserve currency status may raise the activation threshold vs non-reserve-currency sovereigns
The deficit-term premium activation threshold for the US is unknown
Clinton consolidation and Truss expansion are the only available event studies for calibration
10-25% recycling of interest payments as spending creates partial monetary policy offset that complicates analysis
